proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `braf_v600e` = 1 have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `braf_v600e` = 0.
Cell lines with `egfr_amplification` = 1 have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `egfr_amplification` = 0.
Cell lines with `erbb2_amplification` = 1 have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `erbb2_amplification` = 0.
Cell lines with `kras_mutation` = 1 have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `kras_mutation` = 0.
Cell lines with `nf1_loss` = 1 have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `nf1_loss` = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS for `dependency_BRAF` gave a `braf_v600e` coefficient of -0.5545 (n=5574 V600E lines; adjusted for lineage, culture_type, screen_batch, and screen covariates; p < 1e-300).
Adjusted OLS for `dependency_EGFR` gave an `egfr_amplification` coefficient of -0.3489 (n=3880 amplified lines; p < 1e-300).
Adjusted OLS for `dependency_ERBB2` gave an `erbb2_amplification` coefficient of -0.4326 (n=4890 amplified lines; p < 1e-300).
Adjusted OLS for `dependency_KRAS` gave a `kras_mutation` coefficient of -0.3095 (n=11842 mutant lines; p < 1e-300).
Adjusted OLS for `dependency_RIT1` gave an `nf1_loss` coefficient of -0.0954 (n=4560 NF1-loss lines; p=7.87e-69).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among cell lines with `braf_v600e` = 1, `lineage` = skin has more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than non-skin `braf_v600e` = 1 lines.
Among cell lines with `egfr_amplification` = 1, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` >= 0.964 has more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than lower-growth EGFR-amplified lines.
Among cell lines with `erbb2_amplification` = 1, `lineage_subtype` = her2_enriched has more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than other ERBB2-amplified lines.
Among cell lines with `kras_mutation` = 1, `lineage` in {pancreatic, colorectal} has more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than other KRAS-mutant lineages.

analyses.result_summary: Within `braf_v600e` = 1 lines, skin mean `dependency_BRAF` was -0.8114 vs -0.8011 in non-skin mutants; effect=-0.0103, p=0.295, so the skin-specific refinement was not supported.
Within `egfr_amplification` = 1 lines, high-growth lines had mean `dependency_EGFR` -0.6253 vs -0.5930 in lower-growth amplified lines; effect=-0.0322, p=0.0126.
Within `erbb2_amplification` = 1 lines, HER2-enriched lines had mean `dependency_ERBB2` -0.7525 vs -0.6836 in other amplified lines; effect=-0.0689, p=0.0243.

Within `kras_mutation` = 1 lines, pancreatic/colorectal mean `dependency_KRAS` was -0.5547 vs -0.5632 in other mutant lineages; effect=+0.0085, p=0.191, so lineage did not intensify KRAS dependency among mutants.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `lineage` = colorectal have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
Cell lines with `apc_mutation` = 1 have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with `apc_mutation` = 0.
Cell lines with `lineage` = colorectal and `apc_mutation` = 1 have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other lines.
Within colorectal `apc_mutation` = 1 lines, `wnt_activity_score` >= 0.819 has more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than colorectal APC-mutant lines with lower WNT activity.

analyses.result_summary: Colorectal lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.7204 vs -0.2524 in other lineages; effect=-0.4680, p < 1e-300.
APC-mutant lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.5447 vs -0.2526 in APC-wild-type lines; effect=-0.2921, p < 1e-300.
The combined colorectal/APC-mutant subgroup had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.9425 vs -0.2527 in all other lines; effect=-0.6899, p < 1e-300.
Within colorectal APC-mutant lines, WNT-high lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.1119 vs -0.2727 for lower-WNT colorectal APC-mutant lines; effect=-0.8392, p < 1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `lineage` = colorectal, `apc_mutation` = 1, `wnt_activity_score` >= 0.819, and `smad4_loss` = 0 have markedly more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other lines.
Within colorectal `apc_mutation` = 1 and `wnt_activity_score` >= 0.819 lines, `smad4_loss` = 1 suppresses KIF18A dependency, yielding less negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than `smad4_loss` = 0.
Within `lineage` = colorectal, the combination of `apc_mutation` = 1 and `wnt_activity_score` >= 0.819 makes `dependency_KIF18A` more negative than expected from either feature alone.

analyses.result_summary: The complete colorectal/APC/WNT-high/SMAD4-intact subgroup had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.2990 vs -0.2530 in all other lines; effect=-1.0460, p < 1e-300.
After adjustment for lineage, culture_type, screen_batch, and screen covariates, the complete subgroup indicator coefficient for `dependency_KIF18A` remained -1.0380 (p < 1e-300).
Within colorectal/APC/WNT-high lines, SMAD4-loss lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.2557 vs -1.2990 in SMAD4-intact lines; effect=+1.0433, p < 1e-300, supporting suppression by `smad4_loss`.

Within colorectal lines, an OLS model with APC, WNT-high, SMAD4 loss, their interactions, and screen covariates gave an APC:WNT interaction coefficient of -0.9933 for `dependency_KIF18A` (p=5.77e-259).
In the same colorectal interaction model, the APC:WNT:SMAD4-loss coefficient was +0.9958 (p=8.39e-54), showing that SMAD4 loss reverses much of the APC/WNT KIF18A dependency.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` >= 0.964 and `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` >= 5.669 have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than all other lines.
Cell lines with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` >= 0.964 have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than lower-growth lines.
Cell lines with `copy_number_8q24` >= 0.428 and `myc_amplification` = 1 have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than all other lines.

analyses.result_summary: High-growth/high-MYC-expression lines had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.3892 vs -0.3297 in other lines; effect=-0.0595, p=2.48e-21.
Adjusted OLS for the high-growth/high-MYC-expression subgroup retained a negative `dependency_DHX9` coefficient of -0.0339 after lineage and screen-covariate adjustment (p=4.02e-07).
High-growth lines had mean `dependency_POLR2A` -0.4972 vs -0.3928 in lower-growth lines; effect=-0.1043, p=1.01e-179.
The 8q24-high/MYC-amplified subgroup had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.3647 vs -0.2524 in all other lines; effect=-0.1123, p=7.53e-144.
Adjusted OLS for the 8q24-high/MYC-amplified subgroup retained a `dependency_RPL11` coefficient of -0.1074 (p=1.93e-133).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` >= 0.964 and `myc_amplification` = 1 have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than all other lines.
Cell lines with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` >= 0.964, `copy_number_9p21` <= -0.497, and `ifn_gamma_signature` >= 0.519 have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than all other lines.

Cell lines with `myc_amplification` = 1 have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than lines without `myc_amplification`, but the effect is weaker than the combined high-growth/high-MYC-expression subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: High-growth/MYC-amplified lines had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.3936 vs -0.2632 in all other lines; effect=-0.1303, p=1.10e-68.
Adjusted OLS for high-growth/MYC-amplified lines retained a `dependency_RPL11` coefficient of -0.1084 (p=9.37e-47).
The high-growth/9p21-low/IFN-high subgroup had mean `dependency_POLR2A` -0.5413 vs -0.4174 in all other lines; effect=-0.1239, p=1.24e-18.
After lineage and screen-covariate adjustment, this POLR2A subgroup retained a smaller but significant coefficient of -0.0467 (p=6.96e-04).
MYC-amplified lines alone had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.3491 vs -0.3307 without MYC amplification; effect=-0.0184, p=1.69e-05, smaller than the high-growth/high-MYC-expression subgroup effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` >= 0.964 have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than lower-growth lines.

Cell lines with both `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` >= 0.964 and `cas9_activity_score` >= 1.080 have residual, more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores after adjusting for growth rate and Cas9 activity main effects.
Higher `cas9_activity_score` is associated with more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores independent of lineage, culture type, screen batch, and growth rate.

analyses.result_summary: High-growth lines had mean `dependency_TMED10` -0.2816 vs -0.2447 in lower-growth lines; effect=-0.0369, p=2.52e-24.
In an adjusted OLS model, a one-standard-deviation increase in `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` had coefficient -0.0203 for `dependency_TMED10` (p=1.84e-38).

The raw high-growth/high-Cas9 subgroup mean was more negative, but after adjustment for growth and Cas9 main effects the subgroup coefficient was +0.0033 with p=0.646; the residual subgroup hypothesis was refuted.
In the adjusted OLS model, a one-standard-deviation increase in `cas9_activity_score` had coefficient -0.0124 for `dependency_TMED10` (p=1.86e-15).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A systematic subgroup search over binary, categorical, and quartile-threshold features will identify `braf_v600e` = 1 as the dominant subgroup with more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores.

A systematic subgroup search will identify `egfr_amplification` = 1 as the dominant subgroup with more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores, with high growth rate providing only modest additional enrichment.
A systematic subgroup search will identify `erbb2_amplification` = 1 as the dominant subgroup with more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores.
A systematic subgroup search will identify `kras_mutation` = 1 as the dominant subgroup with more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores.
A systematic subgroup search will find that `nf1_loss` = 1 plus `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` <= 4.230 concentrates more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than the full cohort.
A systematic subgroup search will find that `lineage` = colorectal, `apc_mutation` = 1, `wnt_activity_score` >= 0.819, and `smad4_loss` = 0 defines the strongest `dependency_KIF18A` subgroup.
A systematic subgroup search for `dependency_TMED10` will find only broad screen-growth heterogeneity, with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` >= 0.964 more negative than the rest.
A systematic subgroup search will find that `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` >= 0.964 and `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` >= 5.669 concentrates more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores.

A systematic subgroup search will find that `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` >= 0.964 is the most robust `dependency_POLR2A` subgroup, with `copy_number_9p21` <= -0.497 and `ifn_gamma_signature` >= 0.519 further concentrating the effect.
A systematic subgroup search will find that MYC/8q24 context, especially `copy_number_8q24` >= 0.428 and `myc_amplification` = 1, concentrates more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores.

analyses.result_summary: The heterogeneity search ranked `braf_v600e` = 1 as the dominant `dependency_BRAF` subgroup: mean -0.8047 vs -0.2495, effect=-0.5552, p < 1e-300.

The search ranked `egfr_amplification` = 1 as the robust `dependency_EGFR` subgroup: mean -0.6010 vs -0.2524, effect=-0.3486, p < 1e-300. Adding high growth gave a smaller within-amplified enrichment tested in iteration 2.
The search ranked `erbb2_amplification` = 1 as the dominant `dependency_ERBB2` subgroup: mean -0.6856 vs -0.2535, effect=-0.4321, p < 1e-300.
The search ranked `kras_mutation` = 1 as the dominant `dependency_KRAS` subgroup: mean -0.5594 vs -0.2520, effect=-0.3075, p < 1e-300.
The search found `nf1_loss` = 1 and low MYC expression (`rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` <= 4.230) as a concentrated `dependency_RIT1` subgroup: mean -0.3761 vs -0.2633, effect=-0.1128, p=4.27e-26.
The search found the complete colorectal/APC/WNT-high/SMAD4-intact `dependency_KIF18A` subgroup: mean -1.2990 vs -0.2530, effect=-1.0460, p < 1e-300.
The search found broad high-growth `dependency_TMED10` heterogeneity rather than a mutation-defined subgroup: high-growth mean -0.2816 vs -0.2447, effect=-0.0369, p=2.52e-24.
The search found high-growth/high-MYC-expression `dependency_DHX9` enrichment: mean -0.3892 vs -0.3297, effect=-0.0595, p=2.48e-21.
The most robust `dependency_POLR2A` subgroup was high growth alone: mean -0.4972 vs -0.3928, effect=-0.1043, p=1.01e-179.
A more concentrated POLR2A subgroup with high growth, 9p21 low, and IFN-high had mean -0.5413 vs -0.4174, effect=-0.1239, p=1.24e-18.
The search found 8q24-high/MYC-amplified `dependency_RPL11` enrichment: mean -0.3647 vs -0.2524, effect=-0.1123, p=7.53e-144.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The final selected dependency subgroups remain directionally more negative after adjustment for `lineage`, `culture_type`, `screen_batch`, `media_serum_pct`, `cas9_activity_score`, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, `ploidy`, `aneuploidy_score`, and `mutation_burden`, except for subgroup definitions that are themselves only screen-covariate combinations.

analyses.result_summary: Final BRAF subgroup (`braf_v600e` = 1) adjusted coefficient for `dependency_BRAF`: -0.5545, p < 1e-300.
Final EGFR subgroup (`egfr_amplification` = 1) adjusted coefficient for `dependency_EGFR`: -0.3489, p < 1e-300.
Final ERBB2 subgroup (`erbb2_amplification` = 1) adjusted coefficient for `dependency_ERBB2`: -0.4326, p < 1e-300.
Final KRAS subgroup (`kras_mutation` = 1) adjusted coefficient for `dependency_KRAS`: -0.3095, p < 1e-300.
Concentrated RIT1 subgroup (`nf1_loss` = 1 and `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` <= 4.230) adjusted coefficient for `dependency_RIT1`: -0.1130, p=4.19e-27.
Complete KIF18A subgroup adjusted coefficient for `dependency_KIF18A`: -1.0380, p < 1e-300.
High-growth/high-MYC-expression subgroup adjusted coefficient for `dependency_DHX9`: -0.0339, p=4.02e-07.
High-growth/9p21-low/IFN-high subgroup adjusted coefficient for `dependency_POLR2A`: -0.0467, p=6.96e-04.
8q24-high/MYC-amplified subgroup adjusted coefficient for `dependency_RPL11`: -0.1074, p=1.93e-133.
For `dependency_TMED10`, the high-growth/high-Cas9 subgroup did not retain an independent subgroup coefficient after adjusting for growth and Cas9 main effects: +0.0033, p=0.646.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all dependency outcomes, the strongest supported patterns are: BRAF/BRAF V600E, EGFR/EGFR amplification, ERBB2/ERBB2 amplification, KRAS/KRAS mutation, RIT1/NF1 loss with low MYC expression, KIF18A/colorectal APC-mutant WNT-high SMAD4-intact context, TMED10/high growth and Cas9 screen covariates, DHX9/high growth plus high MYC expression, POLR2A/high growth with optional 9p21-low IFN-high concentration, and RPL11/MYC-8q24 context.

analyses.result_summary: The five canonical MAPK/ERBB driver dependencies were all supported in the expected negative direction, with adjusted coefficients from -0.0954 (`nf1_loss` for RIT1) to -0.5545 (`braf_v600e` for BRAF), all p <= 7.87e-69.

The largest observed effect was the KIF18A complete subgroup (`lineage`=colorectal, `apc_mutation`=1, WNT-high, `smad4_loss`=0), with mean contrast -1.0460 and adjusted coefficient -1.0380, p < 1e-300.

Growth-associated outcomes were more diffuse: high growth strongly marked POLR2A (effect=-0.1043, p=1.01e-179), modestly marked TMED10 (effect=-0.0369, p=2.52e-24), and combined with high MYC expression for DHX9 (effect=-0.0595, p=2.48e-21).
RPL11 dependency was best tied to MYC/8q24 biology: the 8q24-high/MYC-amplified subgroup had effect=-0.1123 and adjusted coefficient=-0.1074 (p=1.93e-133 adjusted).
